checks.a_analysis: The output text accurately reflects the content of the source PDF. No fabricated sections, headings, or body text were identified. The structure follows the original document's flow.
The output captures content from all 7 pages of the source document. All primary agenda items and meeting sections are present.

The converted tables for attendees, roll call (members/staff), and guest lists have the correct number of rows matching the source. For example, the guest list correctly contains all 32 names found in the original document.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The 'NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING ROLL CALL VOTE' table, which appears on page 4 and is repeated on page 6, is not converted to a text-based table or fully described. It is represented only as an image. While a summary of the vote result is provided in the text, the specific data record of individual votes for each board member is lost. This constitutes a significant omission of core information (governmental vote records) within the Consent Agenda and Funding Committee sections.
checks.b_evidence_source: NORTH CAROLINA 911 BOARD MEETING ROLL CALL VOTE table containing individual vote records ('Y' or 'Not Present') for 15 board members.
checks.b_evidence_output: Represented as image elements (index 13 and index 18) without text extraction or descriptive alt text for the data contents.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The wording in the converted document is highly faithful to the source. Minor formatting adjustments, such as joining multi-line names, are standard for digital normalization and do not alter the informational content.

checks.a_analysis: The output accurately represents the content of the source document without unintended duplication. Text blocks such as the meeting title, attendee lists, and numbered report sections appear only once in the sequence corresponding to the source document. The 'ROLL CALL VOTE' table image appears twice, but this reflects its presence on both page 4 and page 6 of the source PDF, thus it is an intentional repetition.

The document title 'North Carolina 911 Board Meeting MINUTES' is correctly identified as a heading (level 2). While the numbered sections (1-13) are treated as list items or bold paragraphs rather than heading elements, this is an acceptable structural normalization. There are no instances of skipped levels (e.g., H1 to H3) or incorrect nesting.

Tables in the document are structural complex, involving rowspans (e.g., the 'N/A' cell in the Roll Call table) and sub-headers (e.g., 'STAFF' row in the same table). The JSON output correctly identifies these structures using rowspan and colspan attributes. The conversion of the first page's attendee table, including sub-headers for absent members/staff, is also logically preserved.

The reading order in the JSON output follows the logical flow of the original document, proceeding page-by-page and top-to-bottom. The continuation of section 6 across pages 4 and 5, and sections 9-13 on the final pages, is handled correctly, ensuring the information remains in context.

The numbered report sections (1-13) are appropriately represented as list items. While nested sub-lists (e.g., the lettered items under sections 5, 6, and 8) are flattened into the text content of their parent items, the meaning and logical association of the content are preserved. This is a minor structural simplification that does not impact fidelity.
The source document does not contain any functional hyperlinks or email addresses. The output JSON correctly reflects this by not including any 'link' type elements or fabricated URLs.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML document faithfully reproduces the complex layout of the source PDF. It successfully consolidates multi-page lists (like the guest list) into logical, single tables. The multi-column header on page 1 is correctly transformed into a clean 3-column table. The numbered sections and sub-points are accurately ordered. A minor layout change is the de-duplication of the 'ROLL CALL VOTE' table (which appears on both page 4 and page 6 of the PDF) into a single instance after section 8 in the HTML. This is a logical consolidation that preserves all information and does not confuse the document's meaning.

All tables in the HTML (Meeting Attendees, Roll Call, Guest List, Roll Call Vote) are correctly rendered with clear headers and aligned columns. Row spanning is used effectively for subheaders (e.g., 'Members Absent'). Data within table cells, including 'X' marks and 'N/A' indicators, are accurately placed. Even complex financial lists in the Consent Agenda section are rendered readably.

The meaningful agency logos (NC DIT) present in the source PDF tables are correctly included in the HTML rendering. Informational watermarks (the red 'APPROVED BY...' text) are intentionally omitted for improved accessibility, which is standard practice.

There is no visual duplication in the body of the HTML document that is not already present in the source PDF. For instance, while the guest list is present in two tables, this redundancy exists in the original PDF (page 1 and page 3). The HTML actually performs de-duplication on the 'ROLL CALL VOTE' table, which is an improvement in layout efficiency.
